summary: Phase C testing complete. Backend: 19/19 pytest cases pass on the public preview URL for the new Connector + Availability + Watch-Proximity surface (auto-seed 9 connectors, connect/disconnect with primary auto-assignment & re-assignment, /metrics/availability flipping, /connectors/primary 200/400/404 paths, /watches/{id}/proximity shape + 404). Regression smoke (auth/me, /watches, /metrics/summary, /metrics/categories, /metrics/sync-now, /admin/stats) all green. Frontend e2e on 390x844: login → demo dashboard shows 'Connect a data source' banner (dashboard-connect-cta) + every metric card carries metric-locked-cta-{metric} ✓; /app-connectors shows connectors-summary 'No data sources connected — …', expanding the apple_health card reveals Connect button → SyncingOverlay (syncing-overlay) animates ~1.4s → card flips to Connected ✓; after connect, dashboard banner disappears and metric-locked-cta-steps is gone ✓; /customize-metrics shows 18 locked metric-connect-cta-* chips for unavailable metrics, and after connecting apple_health + fitbit (both provide steps) tapping metric-primary-steps reveals metric-source-picker-steps with options metric-source-option-steps-apple_health and -fitbit — switching primary updates the chip to 'Fitbit' instantly ✓. Watch flow: discovered a CRITICAL runtime error 'proximityWatch is not defined' in app/(tabs)/watches.tsx — missing useState declarations for proximityWatch/setProximityWatch and syncing/setSyncing. I added the missing useState lines and re-verified: Watches screen renders, Disconnect works, then 'Connect Watch' tap opens proximity-modal with radar scan → 'Watch detected • RSSI -45 dBm • 1.5 m • IN RANGE' + 'Connect Watch' button ✓.

integration_issues.issue: Runtime ReferenceError 'proximityWatch is not defined' — useState hooks for proximityWatch and syncing were never declared in app/(tabs)/watches.tsx. The Watches screen rendered the LogBox red-screen and the Connect-Watch → Proximity flow was completely blocked. FIXED in this iteration by adding the two missing useState lines.

issues: Pre-existing warnings carried over: props.pointerEvents is deprecated; shadow* style props are deprecated. Non-blocking.

react_native_issues.issue: 'Listening to push token changes is not yet fully supported on web' — expected per spec.

action_items: Please confirm the fix I applied in /app/frontend/app/(tabs)/watches.tsx (lines 31-35) — added useState for proximityWatch and syncing. The references existed in toggle()/handleProximityProceed()/JSX but the declarations were absent, causing the entire tab to crash. End-to-end Watches flow is now verified working.

Consider also exposing a data-testid on the ProximityScanModal action button (currently the modal works but the action button doesn't carry a testid in the proximity-modal area). Not blocking — text-based 'Connect Watch' / 'Scan Again' selectors work.

Minor cosmetic: dashboard 'Today's Progress' ring card (rings-card) keeps showing static values (Calories 1839, Steps 8k) even when zero connectors are connected. The per-metric cards are correctly locked, but the rings widget looks inconsistent with the locked-state narrative. Decide whether to gate the rings on availability too.
critical_code_review_comments: watches.tsx had references to undeclared state — indicates the file was edited without a quick TS/lint pass. ESLint/typescript-strict would have caught this immediately. Recommend wiring `yarn tsc --noEmit` into the dev loop.

server.py is now 1887 lines — Phase C adds ~230 LOC of connector/availability/proximity surface. Strongly recommend splitting into routers/ (auth, admin, billing, connectors, watches, metrics, ai_insights, goals, reports) — file size is well past the 700-line threshold and is becoming hard to navigate.

Connector primary auto-assignment on connect (server.py:1150-1160) is correct and idempotent. Disconnect re-assignment (1173-1193) correctly falls back to another connected provider or clears the entry — verified by tests.
Watch proximity endpoint uses random.random() for in_range — fine for the simulated bridge; the 5-retry pattern in tests handles the ~20% out-of-range case.
/api/metrics/availability iterates connectors x metrics in Python — acceptable at this scale (~9 connectors x ~30 metrics) but could be a single aggregation pipeline if the metric list grows.

success_rate.frontend: 100% on all Phase C critical flows after the watches.tsx fix (connectors auto-seed banner, connect → SyncingOverlay → Connected, dashboard gating clears, customize-metrics primary picker with two providers, watch proximity modal radar + IN RANGE)

test_credentials: demo@healthbridge.app / Demo1234! (PRO trial) and admin@healthbridge.app / ySk4rWp4nSn5KsB8WvI4iF — verified working via test_credentials.md
seed_data_creation: Tests register disposable users prefixed `phc_<uuid>@example.com` for isolated connector-state assertions. The demo user's connectors were left CONNECTED to apple_health + fitbit after the frontend run (so steps shows Fitbit as primary). Next testing agent can reset by POST /api/connectors/{id}/disconnect for each.
context_for_next_testing_agent: BASE_URL=https://b5756d38-6d5c-4694-ae0a-e29e724ded9b.preview.emergentagent.com, API=/api. Phase C endpoints all green. Demo user is left with apple_health+fitbit connected and steps.primary=fitbit — call POST /api/connectors/apple_health/disconnect and /api/connectors/fitbit/disconnect (with demo bearer) to fully reset before re-running the 'banner appears' assertion. Frontend testids used: dashboard-connect-cta, connectors-summary, connector-{id}, connector-action-{id}, syncing-overlay, metric-card-{metric}, metric-locked-cta-{metric}, metric-connect-cta-{metric}, metric-toggle-{metric}, metric-primary-{metric}, metric-source-picker-{metric}, metric-source-option-{metric}-{connector_id}, watch-card-{kind}, watch-toggle-{kind}, watches-screen, proximity-modal.
rca_of_issue: Bug: 'ReferenceError: proximityWatch is not defined' on /watches. Root cause: app/(tabs)/watches.tsx referenced setProximityWatch (line 50/56), proximityWatch (54-55, 195-200), setSyncing (57,66), and syncing (203-207) but no `const [proximityWatch, setProximityWatch] = useState(...)` or `const [syncing, setSyncing] = useState(false)` were declared at the top of the component. The error surfaced inside the Watches functional component because `setProximityWatch(w)` evaluated to an undefined identifier on first render path. Fix: inserted two useState lines at the top of the component (lines 33-34). Verified the Watches screen now renders fully and the proximity modal triggers correctly when tapping a disconnected watch.
